description: ## Component: hx-accordion (NEW) **Tier 1 — Universal Foundation | Industry Consensus: 5/6 libraries** Vertically stacked expandable/collapsible sections. Composed of hx-accordion-item children. ### ADR-001 Classification: Slot-Driven (Composite) Drupal controls section headings and content via slots. Component provides expand/collapse behavior. ### API Design **Properties:** `multiple` (boolean — allow multiple open), `disabled` **Slots:** Default (hx-accordion-item children) **Events:** `hx-accordion-change` (composed: true, detail: { item, open }) ### Child: hx-accordion-item **Properties:** `open` (boolean), `disabled`, `heading` (string, alt to slot) **Slots:** `heading` (trigger content — rich HTML), default (panel content) **CSS Parts:** `item`, `heading`, `trigger`, `content`, `icon` (expand/collapse chevron) ### Drupal Integration ```twig {# FAQ sections from Drupal Paragraphs #} {% for paragraph in content.field_faq_items %} {{ paragraph.field_question.value }} {{ paragraph.field_answer.value|raw }} {% endfor %} {# Patient intake sections #} Personal Information {{ content.group_personal_info }} Insurance Details {{ content.group_insurance }} ``` ### Healthcare: FAQ sections, patient intake form sections, clinical guidelines, policy documents ### Acceptance Criteria 1. Single mode: only one section open at a time 2. Multiple mode: any combination can be open 3. Smooth height animation on expand/collapse 4. Keyboard: Enter/Space toggle, Arrow keys navigate between headers 5. WCAG 2.1 AA: proper disclosure pattern, aria-expanded 6. slotchange handles dynamic item addition
title: T1-17: hx-accordion — Expandable Sections

descriptionHistory.description: ## Component: hx-accordion (NEW) **Tier 1 — Universal Foundation | Industry Consensus: 5/6 libraries** Vertically stacked expandable/collapsible sections. Composed of hx-accordion-item children. ### ADR-001 Classification: Slot-Driven (Composite) Drupal controls section headings and content via slots. Component provides expand/collapse behavior. ### API Design **Properties:** `multiple` (boolean — allow multiple open), `disabled` **Slots:** Default (hx-accordion-item children) **Events:** `hx-accordion-change` (composed: true, detail: { item, open }) ### Child: hx-accordion-item **Properties:** `open` (boolean), `disabled`, `heading` (string, alt to slot) **Slots:** `heading` (trigger content — rich HTML), default (panel content) **CSS Parts:** `item`, `heading`, `trigger`, `content`, `icon` (expand/collapse chevron) ### Drupal Integration ```twig {# FAQ sections from Drupal Paragraphs #} {% for paragraph in content.field_faq_items %} {{ paragraph.field_question.value }} {{ paragraph.field_answer.value|raw }} {% endfor %} {# Patient intake sections #} Personal Information {{ content.group_personal_info }} Insurance Details {{ content.group_insurance }} ``` ### Healthcare: FAQ sections, patient intake form sections, clinical guidelines, policy documents ### Acceptance Criteria 1. Single mode: only one section open at a time 2. Multiple mode: any combination can be open 3. Smooth height animation on expand/collapse 4. Keyboard: Enter/Space toggle, Arrow keys navigate between headers 5. WCAG 2.1 AA: proper disclosure pattern, aria-expanded 6. slotchange handles dynamic item addition

Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In
`@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-accordion/hx-accordion-item.stories.ts`:
- Around line 81-82: The play function defines an unused variable _canvas from
within(canvasElement); remove the unused assignment or use it consistently —
either delete the const _canvas = within(canvasElement); line in the play
handler for hx-accordion-item.stories.ts (and similarly in
hx-accordion.stories.ts) or rename and use the variable (e.g., canvas =
within(canvasElement)) where interaction queries/assertions occur so there are
no unused variables.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-accordion/hx-accordion-item.styles.ts`:
- Around line 50-64: Update the .trigger selectors to match native button
semantics by adding and/or replacing [aria-disabled='true'] with the :disabled
pseudoclass: change .trigger:hover:not([aria-disabled='true']) to
.trigger:hover:not(:disabled) (or combine both
.trigger:hover:not(:disabled):not([aria-disabled='true']) if you need to support
both), ensure the focus style .trigger:focus-visible maintains behavior for
enabled elements (e.g., .trigger:focus-visible:not(:disabled)), and replace
.trigger[aria-disabled='true'] { cursor: not-allowed; } with .trigger:disabled {
cursor: not-allowed; } (or include both :disabled and [aria-disabled='true']
where backward support is required).
- Around line 6-117: Replace all hardcoded fallback literals in this stylesheet
with the matching --hx-* design tokens: update color fallbacks in .item border,
.trigger background/color, .trigger:hover, .icon color, .content
background/color (replace `#e9ecef`, `#f8f9fa`, `#212529`, `#f1f3f5`, `#ffffff`, etc. with
token vars like --hx-color-*), replace spacing/fallbacks used for padding/size
(var(--hx-space-4), var(--hx-size-5)), typography fallbacks

threadFeedback.agentReasoning:
The feedback points to `::slotted(hx-accordion-item:last-child)` being ineffective across shadow boundaries, and recommends moving the last-item border suppression into `hx-accordion-item.styles.ts` as `:host(:last-of-type) .item { border-bottom: none; }`.

    Both fixes are already implemented in the current codebase:
    1. `hx-accordion.styles.ts` contains no `::slotted` rule — already removed in the iteration 1 refactoring.
    2. `hx-accordion-item.styles.ts` already has the correct `:host(:last-of-type) .item { border-bottom: none; }` rule at lines 25-27.

    The feedback is stale — it was raised against intermediate code that no longer exists. No changes needed.
